symbols: lion lying with ox and lamb — the peaceable kingdom of Isaiah's prophecy
children among wild animals — innocence and trust in the messianic age
Quaker treaty scene in background — the ideal of human peace paralleling the natural peace
leopard and kid together — predator and prey reconciled in God's kingdom
description: A folk-art painting divided into two zones. In the foreground, a large lion, a spotted leopard, a white ox, and other animals lie peacefully together with several small children who sit among them and touch them without fear. A child appears to ride or lean on the lion. In the background at left, a group of figures in colonial-era clothing (Quakers and Native Americans) gather as if conducting a treaty or peaceful meeting. The landscape shows autumn trees and a river. The colors are rich and the style is characteristically naive.
theological_description: Edward Hicks painted over sixty versions of the Peaceable Kingdom, each illustrating Isaiah's messianic prophecy: 'The wolf shall dwell with the lamb, and the leopard shall lie down with the young goat, and the calf and the lion and the fattened calf together; and a little child shall lead them' (Isa 11:6). The foreground literally depicts this vision — children at peace among lions, leopards, and oxen. Isaiah continues: 'They shall not hurt or destroy in all my holy mountain, for the earth shall be full of the knowledge of the Lord' (Isa 11:9). As a Quaker minister, Hicks saw William Penn's treaty with the Lenape people (shown in background) as a historical anticipation of this peaceable kingdom. The painting expresses the hope that the messianic age will bring both natural and human reconciliation. Paul extends this hope cosmically: 'the creation itself will be set free from its bondage to corruption' (Rom 8:21). The painting is both prophetic vision and realized hope.

scripture_refs.reason: The wolf dwells with the lamb, the leopard lies with the kid, a child leads them
The wolf and the lamb shall graze together — the new heavens and new earth
Creation set free from bondage to corruption — cosmic reconciliation
They shall beat their swords into plowshares — the peace treaty in background
